title: Augmenting LLMs to Securely Retrieve Information for Construction and Facility Management
contribution: 提出基于开源组件的RAG系统，用于建筑和设施管理安全信息检索，使用Mixtral 8x7B和Milvus向量数据库，支持多语言处理并保障数据隐私
pdfFile: Krütli et al. - 2025 - Augmenting LLMs to Securely Retrieve Information for Construction and Facility Management.pdf
markdownFile: Krütli et al. - 2025 - Augmenting LLMs to Securely Retrieve Information for Construction and Facility Management.md
abstract: This study introduces a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system tailored to the dynamic needs of facility management. The system aims to provide instant, accurate access to essential information by integrating advanced techniques from natural language processing and information retrieval paradigms. The implementation leverages the Mixtral 8x7B model for multilingual text processing and the Milvus vector database for efficient document storage and retrieval. The dataset includes documents such as images, operation manuals, inspection results, blueprints, and technical drawings in various file formats, reflecting the variety of information encountered in construction and facility management. The evaluation involved generating 30 question-answer pairs pertinent to facility management tasks and assessing the system's performance using metrics such as ROUGE, BLEU, and semantic similarity. The findings suggest that RAG systems can significantly enhance operational efficiency by reducing the time and effort required to access information while maintaining high security and data privacy standards.
methodology: A design science research (DSR) approach following a 5-step process. The system uses exclusively open-source components for security and privacy: (1) PDF document loading and preprocessing using the unstructured Python library with chunk_by_title chunking (max 4000 characters); (2) Enrichment step where Mixtral 8x7B Instruct generates summaries, table descriptions, and keywords for each chunk in German; (3) Embedding using BGE-M3 (multilingual, supports 8192 tokens) to produce both dense and sparse vectors stored in Milvus vector database (one collection per facility); (4) Retrieval via inner product similarity on sparse and dense vectors, combined using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and further reranked by BGE-Reranker-v2-m3 to return the top k chunks; (5) Generation using Mixtral 8x7B Instruct with a German-language prompt template that restricts responses to the provided context only. Evaluation used 30 QA pairs generated by GPT-4 from facility management documents across 3 facilities, assessed via ROUGE-1/ROUGE-2 F1, BLEU, semantic similarity (BGE-Reranker), and ChatGPT-based human-like qualitative scoring (accuracy, relevance, coherence, fluency on 1-10 scale).
results: Quantitative results on 30 QA pairs: average ROUGE-1 F1 = 0.3486, ROUGE-2 F1 = 0.1602, BLEU = 0.0879, semantic similarity = 0.9409. Semantic similarity was generally high (most answers above 0.99), indicating strong content understanding, though lexical precision (ROUGE/BLEU) showed more variability. Best-performing answer (Q2) achieved ROUGE-1 F1 of 0.625 and semantic similarity of 1.0. Qualitative ChatGPT-based evaluation averages: accuracy = 7.30/10, relevance = 8.13/10, coherence = 7.83/10, fluency = 8.80/10. 60% of answers (18/30) scored above 7 for accuracy; 83% (25/30) scored above 7 for relevance; 97% (29/30) scored above 7 for fluency. Key issues identified: (1) some answers contained hallucinated information when relevant context was missing from retrieved chunks (e.g., answer 6); (2) several answers were abruptly truncated due to a likely max_tokens misconfiguration; (3) preprocessing of HTML tables into continuous text sometimes lost critical structured data.
